quick_skim.title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for coupled stiff transport systems
quick_skim.abstract: Purpose: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) struggle with stiff, regime-changing transport equations due to instability, loss imbalance, and violations of physical consistency. This paper investigates these failures through the Marshak wave equations - a canonical benchmark from radiative transport - where initial and boundary conditions differ by up to 12 orders of magnitude, and proposes targeted modifications to the standard PINN framework to overcome them.   Design/methodology/approach: Three modifications are introduced: (1) a ScaledSigmoid final activation enforcing physical bounds and positivity of the unknowns; (2) a logarithmic MSE loss replacing the standard quadratic loss for initial and boundary conditions, enabling training across extreme scale disparities; and (3) explicit enforcement of global conservation laws derived from the governing equations as an additional physics loss term. Monte Carlo sampling with exponential time weighting is used throughout.   Findings: The proposed framework successfully recovers the Marshak wave dynamics - including the hot, cold, and wave-front regions - in agreement with a reference Implicit Monte Carlo solution, with run times under 30 minutes. Ablation studies confirm that each ingredient is essential: linear activation, absence of the logarithmic loss, or removal of the PDE term each independently cause the method to fail qualitatively.   Originality/value: This work identifies and resolves three concrete failure modes of standard PINNs on stiff hyperbolic systems with nonlinear coupling. The combination of bounded activations, scale-aware loss functions, and conservation law enforcement constitutes a novel and practically validated framework, with applicability to radiative transport and other coupled stiff PDE systems in engineering.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Proposes modifications to PINNs for stiff transport equations directly applicable to environmental transport modeling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对刚性输运方程改进PINN，直接适用于环境输运建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对刚性输运方程改进PINN，直接适用于环境输运建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对刚性输运方程改进PINN，直接适用于环境输运建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Addresses PINNs failures on stiff transport equations through three modifications: scaled sigmoid activation, logarithmic MSE loss, and adaptive weighting. Demonstrated on Marshak wave equations, the method improves stability and accuracy, transferable to atmospheric chemical transport modeling.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对物理信息神经网络（PINN）在刚性输运方程上不稳定、损失不平衡等问题，以辐射输运中的Marshak波方程为例，提出三项改进：缩放Sigmoid激活函数确保物理边界、对数均方损失处理量级差异、自适应加权。实验表明这些修改显著提升训练稳定性和精度，方法可直接推广至大气化学输运方程等环境建模任务，为物理信息机器学习在空气质量模型中的应用提供了关键工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对物理信息神经网络（PINN）在刚性输运方程上不稳定、损失不平衡等问题，以辐射输运中的Marshak波方程为例，提出三项改进：缩放Sigmoid激活函数确保物理边界、对数均方损失处理量级差异、自适应加权。实验表明这些修改显著提升训练稳定性和精度，方法可直接推广至大气化学输运方程等环境建模任务，为物理信息机器学习在空气质量模型中的应用提供了关键工具。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: physics-informed machine learning for environmental modeling

quick_skim.title: Neural Conjugate Aggregation: Identifiable Unsupervised Multi-Sensor Regression under Heterogeneous Sensor Bias
quick_skim.abstract: We study regression-based data fusion under uncertainty, where multiple noisy and biased measurement sources are available but ground-truth labels are absent during training. This setting arises in sensor networks, simulation ensembles, and scientific monitoring systems where supervision is costly or infeasible. We propose the Neural Conjugate Aggregation Model (NCAM), a hierarchical Bayesian framework that combines neural networks with conjugate Gaussian inference for unsupervised multi-source fusion. NCAM learns source-specific bias and reliability conditioned on contextual covariates, yielding an analytically tractable posterior over a latent target variable with decomposed epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty. Structural non-identifiability is resolved through sensor anchoring and variance regularization, enabling stable and interpretable posterior aggregation. To complement Bayesian uncertainty with finite-sample guarantees, we integrate locally adaptive Monte Carlo conformal prediction, producing heteroscedastic prediction intervals with coverage guarantees under exchangeability assumptions. Experiments on synthetic and real-world air-quality datasets demonstrate improved predictive accuracy and well-calibrated uncertainty compared to unsupervised baselines, including mean aggregation, probabilistic PCA, and Kalman filtering.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: unsupervised multi-sensor fusion with bias correction and uncertainty decomposition for data assimilation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 无监督多传感器融合，包含偏差校正和不确定性分解，可用于数据同化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 无监督多传感器融合，包含偏差校正和不确定性分解，可用于数据同化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 无监督多传感器融合，包含偏差校正和不确定性分解，可用于数据同化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Neural Conjugate Aggregation Model (NCAM) for unsupervised multi-source fusion with learned bias and reliability, providing analytically tractable posterior and uncertainty decomposition.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多源观测数据融合中缺乏真值标签且存在传感器偏差的问题，提出神经共轭聚合模型（NCAM），结合神经网络与共轭高斯推断，在无监督条件下学习传感器偏差和可靠性，并得到潜变量的解析后验，分解认知和偶然不确定性。该方法适用于传感器网络和模拟集合，可用于空气质量数据同化中的偏差校正与融合，提升预测可靠性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对多源观测数据融合中缺乏真值标签且存在传感器偏差的问题，提出神经共轭聚合模型（NCAM），结合神经网络与共轭高斯推断，在无监督条件下学习传感器偏差和可靠性，并得到潜变量的解析后验，分解认知和偶然不确定性。该方法适用于传感器网络和模拟集合，可用于空气质量数据同化中的偏差校正与融合，提升预测可靠性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: deep learning methods for PM2.5 ozone air quality forecasting bias correction and data assimilation
